name: Pelvic Curl
setup: Lying supine in neutral spine position, legs parallel, knees bent, feet placed firmly on Mat, arms by sides
exhale: Roll pelvis and spine up
inhale: Pause
cues: Draw abdominals in prior to movement
Maximize lumbar flexion as pelvis and spine roll up and down
Articulate through each vertebra as spine rolls up and down
Return to neutral spine after each repetition

name: Spine Twist Supine
setup: Lying supine, lower back imprinted, arms in T position, palms facing up, legs in tabletop position (hips and knees at 90 degrees)
inhale: Lower legs to one side
exhale: Lift legs to center, return to start position
cues: Keep shoulders relaxed and scapulae on Mat
Move legs and pelvis as one unit
Initiate rotation with abdominals

name: Chest Lift
setup: Lying supine in neutral spine position, legs parallel, knees bent, feet hip width apart, fingers interlaced behind head
exhale: Lift head and chest
inhale: Pause
cues: Maintain pelvic stabilization
Keep head aligned with spine when lifting into spinal flexion
Allow head to rest in hands
Engage hip adductors throughout

name: Chest Lift with Rotation
setup: Starting in Chest Lift position
exhale: Rotate to one side
inhale: Return to center
cues: Maintain pelvic stabilization
Maximize rotation while avoiding lateral flexion
Move head, shoulders and chest together as one unit
Maintain trunk flexion at consistent height

name: Single Leg Lift
setup: Lying supine in neutral spine position, knees bent, feet placed firmly on Mat, arms by sides
exhale: Lift one leg, plantarflex foot
inhale: Lower leg, touch Mat with toes
cues: Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization
Keep shoulders relaxed
Maintain consistent angle of knee joint (90 degrees)

name: Leg Changes
setup: Starting in Single Leg Lift position
inhale: Lift one leg, foot plantarflexed
exhale: Switch legs
cues: Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization
Keep shoulders relaxed
Maintain consistent angle of knee joint (90 degrees)

name: Hundred Prep
setup: Lying supine, legs in tabletop position, arms overhead, palms facing up
exhale: Lift arms, head and chest, bringing arms to sides
inhale: Lift arms to 90 degrees, lower chest and head, return to start position
cues: Move head and chest as one unit
Maintain pelvic and lumbar stabilization
Minimize neck tension
Maintain tabletop position with legs

name: Roll Up
setup: Lying supine in neutral spine position, arms overhead, palms facing each other, legs straight and together, feet plantarflexed
inhale: Lift arms up and forward, followed by head and chest; pause
exhale: Roll up to eyes over knees; roll down, return to start position
cues: Pause briefly in Chest Lift position
Maintain C curve when rolling up and rolling down
Pause when shoulders are in front of hip joints and eyes are over knee joints
Keep head aligned with spine

name: Leg Circles
setup: Lying supine, arms down by sides or in T position, one leg straight up to ceiling, foot dorsiflexed, other leg on Mat, foot plantarflexed
inhale: Bring leg across center line, circle down and around
exhale: Complete circle, same direction
cues: Keep shoulders stable
Maintain fluid movement
Keep circles within range of pelvic lumbar control

name: Roll-Like-a-Ball
setup: Sitting in balanced position, body in ball shape, legs bent, hands on shins close to ankles, feet off Mat
inhale: Roll back
exhale: Roll forward, return to start position
cues: Maintain fluid movement
Keep ball shape consistent
Keep head aligned with spine
Keep pulling legs toward chest and heels toward sit bones

name: Spine Stretch
setup: Sitting upright, legs straight, feet dorsiflexed, shoulder width apart, arms straight and reaching forward parallel to Mat, palms facing each other
inhale: Prepare; pause at bottom
exhale: Roll down and forward through spine; roll up through spine, return to start position
cues: Keep toes facing ceiling throughout
Maintain scapular stabilization
Articulate spine as if rolling down and up a wall
Following roll down tip pelvis forward; prior to roll up return pelvis to upright position

name: Side Lifts
setup: Lying on side in neutral spine position, bottom arm straight and palm facing up, head resting on arm, pelvis perpendicular to Mat, legs together, top hand placed on Mat with elbow bent
exhale: Lift legs
inhale: Lower legs, without touching Mat
cues: Bias work toward abdominals (move legs slightly forward of center line if necessary)
Keep neck and shoulders relaxed
Move legs as one unit when tilting pelvis

name: Back Extension
setup: Lying prone, head aligned with spine, arms straight and by sides pressing against legs
inhale: Lift upper trunk
exhale: Lower upper trunk, without touching Mat
cues: Keep head aligned with spine
Keep legs together
Maintain abdominal support

name: Rest Position
setup: Kneeling with legs together, pelvis resting on heels, chest resting on thighs, arms reaching forward, neck relaxed
exhale: Breathe freely, relaxing back, neck and shoulders
cues: Allow pelvis to sink towards heels
Visualize lumbar vertebra opening
Breathe fully and relax deeper with each exhalation

name: Hundred
setup: Starting in Hundred Prep position
exhale: Lift arms, head and chest, bringing arms to sides of body parallel to Mat, simultaneously straighten legs forward; pump arms up and down for 5 counts; final exhale: lift arms to 90 degrees, lower body to start
inhale: Pause; pump arms up and down for 5 counts (continue for 10 breath cycles)
cues: Keep pumps of arms small
Keep legs stable at safe height
Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization

name: Double Leg Stretch
setup: Lying supine, legs bent, hands on knees, shins parallel to Mat, trunk in Chest Lift position
inhale: Reach arms overhead, straighten legs forward
exhale: Circle arms around, simultaneously bend legs toward chest, return to start position
cues: Keep head aligned with spine
Reach arms as far back as possible
Avoid scapular elevation
Maintain consistent height of trunk

name: Single Leg Stretch
setup: Starting in Double Leg Stretch position
exhale: Straighten one leg, inside hand on bent knee, outside hand on top of other hand
inhale: Switch legs and hands; final inhale: bend both legs, return to start position
cues: Maintain trunk flexion at consistent height
Keep feet at same height
Keep shin of bent leg parallel to Mat
Use arms to support position

name: Criss Cross
setup: Starting in Double Leg Stretch position, place hands behind head, fingers interlaced
exhale: Straighten one leg, rotate toward bent knee
inhale: Switch legs; final inhale: bend both legs, return to start position
cues: Maintain trunk flexion at consistent height
Maximize engagement of abdominal obliques
Maximize rotation while avoiding lateral flexion
Keep elbows wide

name: Saw
setup: Sitting upright, legs straight, feet dorsiflexed shoulder width apart, arms in T position, palms facing forward
inhale: Rotate trunk
exhale: Reach forward as back arm internally rotates, front hand reaches past outside of foot; inhale return to upright in rotation; exhale rotate to center, return to start position
cues: Rotate around longitudinal axis
Maintain back extensor engagement
Keep head aligned with spine, nose aligned with sternum

name: Spine Twist
setup: Sitting upright, legs straight and together, feet dorsiflexed, toes toward ceiling, arms in T position, palms facing up
exhale: Rotate trunk to one side (2 pulses)
inhale: Rotate to center
cues: Keep pelvis and legs stable throughout
Move head and arms together with trunk
Initiate movement from trunk and not arms
Maintain T position with arms
Keep head aligned with spine, nose aligned with sternum

name: Corkscrew
setup: Lying supine, arms by sides, legs straight and together, perpendicular to Mat
inhale: Move legs to one side
exhale: Circle legs down and around to opposite side, return to start position; alternate direction
cues: Move pelvis and legs as one unit
Keep legs together
Initiate movement with spinal rotators
Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization (bend knees if necessary)

name: Single Leg Kick
setup: Lying prone, trunk lifted, arms bent, elbows under shoulders, fingers interlaced, legs straight and together, lifted off Mat
exhale: Bend leg (2 pulses), switch legs (2 pulses)
inhale: Bend leg (2 pulses), switch legs (2 pulses); final inhale: straighten legs, return to start position
cues: Maintain scapular stabilization
Keep abdominals engaged
Keep both legs lifted off Mat
Press elbows into Mat to assist thoracic extension

name: Cat Stretch
setup: Kneeling in quadruped position, neutral spine, wrists under shoulders, knees under hips
exhale: Round spine, return to start position
inhale: Extend spine, return to start position
cues: Focus flexion in lumbar spine
Focus extension in thoracic spine
Keep pelvic lumbar region stable during thoracic extension
Maintain scapular stabilization throughout

name: Front Support
setup: Starting in plank position, arms straight and parallel, wrists under shoulders, legs straight and parallel, feet hip width apart
exhale: Bend one leg toward chest
inhale: Straighten leg back to start position; bend opposite leg toward chest; straighten leg back
cues: Keep body in straight diagonal line
Keep abdominals engaged to avoid sinking in lower back
Focus on hip disassociation

name: Back Support
setup: Sitting, arms straight and parallel, hands placed on Mat behind pelvis, fingers facing feet, legs straight and parallel, feet plantarflexed, hip width apart
exhale: Lift pelvis (Back Support position)
inhale: Lower pelvis, without touching Mat
cues: Create straight diagonal line when pelvis is lifted
Hinge at hip joint when lifting and lowering pelvis
Move trunk as one unit
If there is discomfort in wrists, face fingers out to sides or make fists

name: Seal Puppy
setup: Sitting in balanced position with body in ball shape, legs bent, arms wrapped under legs, feet plantarflexed, palms on top of feet
inhale: Roll back, clap feet together three times
exhale: Roll up, return to start position, clap feet together three times
cues: Keep eyes forward
Maintain scapular stabilization
Maintain consistent curve of spine
Roll onto shoulders, avoid rolling onto neck

name: Hamstring Pull 1
setup: Starting in Chest Lift position, legs together, perpendicular to Mat, hands behind calves
exhale: Lower one leg to Mat, lift other leg toward chest holding calf (2 pulses)
inhale: Switch legs
cues: Pull gently on leg during pulses
Maintain stability of trunk and pelvis as legs change
Anchor bottom leg on Mat and keep stable (no pulses)
Maintain consistent height of trunk

name: Roll Over
setup: Lying supine, arms by sides of body, legs straight and together, on diagonal line (approximately 60 degrees), feet plantarflexed
inhale: Lift legs perpendicular to Mat; dorsiflex feet, separate legs shoulder width apart, lower feet toward Mat
exhale: Roll over, legs parallel to Mat; roll down to pelvis, plantarflex feet, lower legs to diagonal line, bring together, return to start
cues: Use spinal flexion for roll over
Maintain 90 degree angle in hip joint during roll over
Keep thighs close to chest when rolling back down
Plantarflex feet when legs are perpendicular to Mat following roll down

name: Open Leg Rocker
setup: Sitting in balanced position, back flat, one hand holding each leg close to ankles, legs shoulder width apart, arms straight, feet plantarflexed
inhale: Round back, roll back
exhale: Roll up, straighten back, return to start position
cues: Keep arms straight
Initiate roll back with flexion of lumbar spine
Utilize spinal flexors to initiate movement
Utilize spinal extensors to complete movement
Maintain scapular stabilization

name: Shoulder Bridge Prep
setup: Starting in up phase of Pelvic Curl
exhale: Lift one leg toward chest, plantarflex foot
inhale: Lower leg, touching Mat with toes
cues: Keep hip adductors engaged
Maintain 90 degree angle in knee of moving leg
Maintain hip extension with supporting leg
Keep pelvis lifted, level, and stable
Keep arms pressing into Mat

name: Side Kick
setup: Lying on side, leaning on elbow, fingers touching sides of head, trunk lifted, legs straight and aligned with body, top leg at hip height, feet plantarflexed
exhale: Swing top leg forward, dorsiflex foot (2 pulses)
inhale: Swing top leg back, plantarflex foot (2 pulses)
cues: Maintain neutral pelvis (as close as possible)
Maintain stable trunk
Avoid sinking into supporting shoulder and rib cage
Keep head aligned with spine

name: Double Leg Kick
setup: Lying prone, legs straight and together, lifted off Mat, fingers interlaced behind back, arms relaxed, head to side, cheek resting on Mat
exhale: Bend both legs (3 pulses); lower trunk, bend arms and legs, turn head to opposite side (3 pulses)
inhale: Extend back, turn head to center, straighten legs and arms
cues: Maintain scapular stabilization
Reach elbows to Mat when hands are resting on back
Keep legs lifted off Mat

name: Swimming
setup: Lying prone, arms straight and reaching forward, legs straight and reaching back, chest, arms and legs lifted off Mat, head between arms
inhale: Lift right arm and left leg, left arm and right leg continue alternating (5 counts)
exhale: Continue alternating movement pattern (5 counts)
cues: Maintain scapular stabilization
Maintain trunk stabilization
Focus on cross-pattern coordination of arms and legs
Keep head aligned with spine

name: Rocking Prep
setup: Lying prone, knees bent, arms straight and reaching back, feet plantarflexed, hands holding feet
inhale: Lift trunk and legs toward ceiling
exhale: Lower trunk and legs to Mat, return to start position
cues: Reach toes toward ceiling
Keep head aligned with spine
Extend hips, knees and back simultaneously
Maintain scapular stabilization

name: Leg Pull Front
setup: Starting in Front Support position, one leg lifted slightly off Mat, foot plantarflexed
exhale: Lift leg
inhale: Lower leg
cues: Keep body in straight diagonal line
Keep pelvis in neutral position or slight posterior tilt
Maintain scapular stabilization
Focus on hip disassociation when lifting and lowering leg

name: Leg Pull Back
setup: Starting in Back Support position
exhale: Lift leg
inhale: Lower leg
cues: Keep spinal extensors engaged
Keep head aligned with spine
Focus on hip disassociation when lifting and lowering leg
Keep body in straight diagonal line

name: Teaser Prep
setup: Sitting in balanced position, back flat, arms straight and reaching forward parallel to Mat, legs lifted, knees bent, shins parallel to Mat
exhale: Roll down
inhale: Roll up, return to start position
cues: Engage spinal extensors to complete movement
Articulate through spine when rolling down and up
Initiate roll down and roll up with lumbar flexion
Keep arms parallel to Mat

name: Hamstring Pull 2
setup: Starting in Hamstring Pull 1 position, fingers interlaced behind head
exhale: Lower one leg to Mat, lift other leg toward chest (2 pulses)
inhale: Switch legs
cues: Support head with hands
Maintain stability of trunk and pelvis as legs change
Anchor bottom leg on Mat and keep stable (no pulses)
Maintain consistent height of trunk

name: Hamstring Pull 3
setup: Starting in Hamstring Pull 2 position
exhale: Lower one leg to Mat, lift other leg toward chest, rotate trunk toward lifted leg (2 pulses)
inhale: Switch
cues: Maximize rotation while avoiding lateral flexion
Maintain stability of trunk and pelvis as legs change
Anchor bottom leg on Mat and keep stable (no pulses)
Maintain consistent height of trunk

name: Shoulder Bridge
setup: Starting in up phase of Pelvic Curl, one leg straight, foot plantarflexed, reaching toward ceiling
exhale: Lower leg
inhale: Lift leg, dorsiflex foot
cues: Stretch front of straight leg during lowering phase
Stretch back of straight leg during lifting phase
Maintain consistent height of pelvis
Bias toward posterior tilt of pelvis
Keep pelvis level

name: Control Balance
setup: Starting in Roll Over position, feet dorsiflexed and anchored on Mat, arms straight and overhead, hands holding feet (or calves)
exhale: Lift one leg toward ceiling (2 pulses)
inhale: Switch legs
cues: Keep weight on shoulder girdle
Minimize weight on cervical spine
Switch legs simultaneously
Maintain pelvic stabilization as legs change

name: Jackknife
setup: Starting in Roll Over position
inhale: Lift legs perpendicular to Mat; lower legs, lift legs toward ceiling
exhale: Roll over; roll down, return to start position
cues: Touch feet to Mat (as close as possible) prior to lifting
Create vertical line with body when legs lift to ceiling
Utilize hip, back and shoulder extensors to achieve vertical line
Roll down with feet opposite face

name: Hip Circles Prep
setup: Sitting in V position, arms straight and parallel, hands placed on Mat behind pelvis, fingers facing away from body, legs straight and together on diagonal line, feet plantarflexed
inhale: Move legs to one side
exhale: Circle legs down and around to opposite side, return to start position; change direction after 5 repetitions
cues: Move pelvis and legs as one unit
Keep legs together
Initiate movement with spinal rotators
Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization (bend knees if necessary)

name: Side Kick Kneeling
setup: Kneeling on one knee, shoulder over wrist, fingers facing away from body, top leg lifted to hip height (or higher), fingers touching behind head
exhale: Swing top leg forward, dorsiflex foot (2 pulses)
inhale: Swing top leg back, plantarflex foot (2 pulses)
cues: Maintain neutral pelvis (as close as possible)
Maintain stable trunk
Avoid sinking into supporting shoulder and rib cage
Keep head aligned with spine

name: Side Bend
setup: Sitting sideways, weight on one side of pelvis and supporting arm, legs bent, top leg in front of bottom leg, other arm down by side
inhale: Lift pelvis, straighten legs, raise top arm to shoulder height, body on diagonal line; return to diagonal line
exhale: Lift pelvis higher toward ceiling, transfer top arm overhead; lower body, return to start position
cues: Keep legs together and weight on foot of top leg
Keep bottom foot plantarflexed and resting on Mat
Maximize use of lateral flexors and minimize use of legs

name: Swan Dive Prep
setup: Lying prone, elbows close to sides of body, forearms pressing into Mat
inhale: Lift trunk, straighten arms
exhale: Lower trunk, bend arms, simultaneously lift legs off Mat
cues: Maintain abdominal engagement throughout
Lift trunk as high as possible prior to lifting forearms and straightening arms
Keep elbows close to sides of body
Maintain scapular stabilization

name: Teaser 1
setup: Balance in V position, legs straight and together on diagonal line, back flat, arms straight and overhead, shoulder width apart
exhale: Roll down, lift arms overhead
inhale: Roll up, return to start position
cues: Keep legs at consistent angle
Articulate through spine when rolling down and up
Keep arms at shoulder height prior to reaching overhead following roll down and roll up

name: Neck Pull
setup: Lying supine, hands interlaced behind head, legs straight and together, feet plantarflexed
inhale: Lift head and chest; articulate spine to upright position, hinge back
exhale: Roll up, bring trunk over thighs; roll down, return to start position
cues: Keep elbows wide
Avoid pulling on neck when rolling up
Hinge trunk back to diagonal line prior to rolling down

name: Scissors
setup: Resting on shoulder girdle and upper arms, arms bent and parallel, hands holding pelvis, back arched, legs straight and together, feet plantarflexed, reaching toward ceiling
exhale: Open legs to Scissors position (2 pulses)
inhale: Switch
cues: Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization
Pass through the perpendicular position following each repetition
Create even V when legs are in Scissors position

name: Bicycle
setup: Starting in Scissors position, bend back knee toward Mat
exhale: Bicycle legs: lower one leg, bend knee to 90 degrees bringing toward chest, straighten to V; simultaneously repeat with other leg (3 cycles each direction)
inhale: Continue alternating bicycle pattern
cues: Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization
Create even V when legs are in Scissors position
Avoid externally rotating hip when bending back knee
Touch Mat with foot of bent leg in both directions (as close as possible)

name: Corkscrew Advanced
setup: Starting in Roll Over position, legs overhead
inhale: Rotate spine, move legs to one side
exhale: Roll down side of trunk, circle legs around, rotate spine, roll up opposite side of trunk, move legs to center, return to start position; alternate sides
cues: Move pelvis and legs as one unit
Initiate movement with spinal rotators
Keep shoulders stable
Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization
Imagine drawing a large circle with feet around body

name: Rocking
setup: Starting in lifted position of Rocking Prep
exhale: Rock forward, lift feet toward ceiling
inhale: Rock back, lift top of head toward ceiling
cues: Initiate rock forward with hip extensor activation
Keep head aligned with spine
Keep scapulae open and chest wide
Maintain consistent shape of body throughout

name: Teaser 2
setup: Starting in Teaser 1 position
inhale: Lower legs
exhale: Lift legs
cues: Maintain stable trunk throughout
Keep shoulders relaxed
Keep movement of legs controlled

name: Teaser 3
setup: Starting in Teaser 1 position
exhale: Lower trunk and legs simultaneously, reach arms overhead
inhale: Lift arms, trunk and legs, return to start position
cues: Lower legs to slightly above Mat
Articulate through spine when rolling down and rolling up
Keep arms at shoulder height prior to reaching overhead following roll down and roll up

name: Hip Circles
setup: Balancing in V position, legs straight and together on diagonal line, back flat, arms straight and reaching forward, shoulder width apart
inhale: Rotate upper trunk, arms, and head in one direction, pelvis and legs in opposite direction
exhale: Circle legs down and around to opposite side and arms up and around to opposite side simultaneously, return to start position; change direction after 5 repetitions
cues: Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization
Move legs and pelvis as one unit
Move arms, trunk and head as one unit

name: Twist
setup: Starting in Side Bend position
inhale: Lift pelvis, straighten legs, raise top arm to shoulder height, body on diagonal line; return to diagonal line
exhale: Lift pelvis toward ceiling, rotate trunk, reach arm under and back; lower body, return to start position
cues: Keep legs together and weight on foot of top leg
Create pyramid shape when lifting pelvis toward ceiling
Maximize use of lateral flexors and minimize use of legs
Reach arm back maximizing spinal rotation

name: Push Up
setup: Starting in Front Support position
inhale: Bend arms; pause
exhale: Straighten arms; lift pelvis, walk hands toward feet, roll up; roll down, walk hands forward, return to start position
cues: Keep elbows close to sides of body when bending
Maintain scapular stabilization
Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization, bias toward posterior tilt of pelvis

name: Swan Dive Prep with Catch
setup: Starting in lifted position of Swan Dive Prep
exhale: Drop trunk forward, place hands under forehead, lift legs toward ceiling
inhale: Lift trunk, lower legs, place hands on Mat, return to start position
cues: Maintain consistent arc shape
Engage abdominals throughout
Maximize fluidity of movement

name: Swan Dive
setup: Starting in lifted position of Swan Dive Prep
exhale: Drop trunk forward, place hands under forehead, lift legs toward ceiling; drop trunk forward, reach arms overhead in line with shoulders, lift legs toward ceiling
inhale: Lift trunk, lower legs, reach arms overhead toward ceiling; final inhale: lift trunk, lower legs, place hands on Mat, return to start position
cues: Maintain consistent arc shape
Engage abdominals throughout
Maximize fluidity of movement

name: Boomerang
setup: Sitting with legs straight and crossed, trunk over legs, arms straight and reaching forward
inhale: Roll down, lift legs; switch legs; circle arms around, interlace fingers behind back
exhale: Roll over; roll up into Teaser 1 position, legs crossed; lower legs, reach trunk over legs, circle arms forward, return to start position
cues: Keep movement fluid
Maximize stretch for shoulders and chest
Create Boomerang position (BASI Rock) in Chest Lift position, legs lifted

name: Crab
setup: Sitting in balanced position, trunk in C curve position, legs bent ankles crossed, feet plantarflexed, arms reaching around legs, hands holding feet, thumbs underfoot, fingers on top
exhale: Roll back; roll forward over legs, place head on Mat, transfer weight toward head
inhale: Straighten legs, switch legs, bend knees; sit back on sit bones, return to start position
cues: Maximize spinal flexion in lumbar spine
Maintain consistent C curve position
Push hands into Mat when rolling over legs
Transfer weight cautiously onto head

name: Side Leg Lifts
setup: Lying on side in neutral spine position, bottom arm straight and palm facing up, head resting on arm, pelvis perpendicular to Mat, top hand placed on Mat with elbow bent, top leg straight at hip height in line with trunk, hip internally rotated
exhale: Lift leg
inhale: Lower leg, return to start position
cues: Keep knee slightly bent
Keep pelvis stable as leg moves up and down
Keep leg and foot relaxed, toes directed toward Mat

name: Forward and Lift
setup: Starting in Side Leg Lifts position
exhale: Move leg forward to 90 degree hip flexion, lift leg, lower leg
inhale: Move leg back, return to start position
cues: Keep pelvis stable as leg moves forward and back, up and down
Keep knee slightly bent
Keep leg and foot relaxed, toes directed toward Mat
Keep movement hip height and higher

name: Forward with Drops
setup: Starting in Side Leg Lifts position, leg brought forward to 90 degrees hip flexion
exhale: Lower leg, lift leg (5 pulses)
inhale: Lower leg, lift leg (5 pulses)
cues: Keep pelvis stable as leg moves down and up
Keep knee slightly bent
Make movement small
Keep leg and foot relaxed, toes directed toward Mat
Keep movement hip height and lower

name: Adductor Lift
setup: Starting in Side Leg Lifts position, top leg in front of bottom leg, hip externally rotated, bottom leg straight and parallel, foot plantarflexed
exhale: Lift leg
inhale: Lower leg
cues: Keep pelvis perpendicular to Mat
Maintain neutral spine position throughout
Keep bent leg stable, knee reaching toward ceiling
Avoid resting leg on Mat between repetitions

name: Hip Extension Bent Knee
setup: Kneeling in quadruped position, neutral spine, wrists under shoulders, knees under hips
exhale: Lift leg
inhale: Lower leg, return to start position
cues: Focus on hip disassociation
Maintain pelvic stabilization with emphasis on avoiding anterior tilt and rotation of pelvis
Reach toes to ceiling
Maintain consistent 90 degree angle in knee of moving leg

name: Hip Abduction Bent Knee
setup: Starting in Hip Extension Bent Knee position
exhale: Lift leg to side
inhale: Lower leg, return to start position
cues: Focus on hip disassociation
Maintain pelvic stabilization with emphasis on avoiding lateral tilt and rotation of pelvis
Minimize shifting of weight
Keep shoulders aligned over wrists
Maintain consistent 90 degree angle in knee of moving leg

name: Hip Extension Straight Leg
setup: Starting in Hip Extension Bent Knee position, one leg straight back, foot plantarflexed
exhale: Lift leg
inhale: Lower leg, return to start position
cues: Focus on hip disassociation
Maintain pelvic stabilization with emphasis on avoiding anterior tilt and rotation of pelvis
Keep leg elongated
